discovery.run_summary: Risk-on tape driven by semis (SMH +4.5%, AVGO +3.8%, AAPL +2.2%) with memory/CPU rotation narrative continuing. Earnings-heavy session creating dispersion in mid-caps: clean beats (DBX, SYNA, MXL, TXRH) ripping double-digits while disappointments (FLR, GMED, OSK, KMT, LFST) get hammered. VIX up modestly despite rally hints at hedging into Trump-Xi/EU tariff headlines.

notable_index_moves: SMH +4.47% and XLK +3.11% — semi-led rally with memory/CPU rotation (Micron, AMD, Intel) per news
XLV -0.89% lagging — healthcare disappointments (GMED, LFST) weighing
VIX +1.7% rising alongside SPY +0.75% — unusual; suggests hedging into EU tariff deadline rhetoric
DIA flat vs QQQ +2.1% — narrow tech-led tape, not broad participation

discoveries.name: Fluor Corporation
discoveries.setup: Earnings reaction with 5-day decline already -18%, suggesting pre-positioning followed by capitulation
discoveries.thesis: Q1 print likely missed on margin or NuScale-related items; -15% on 3.6x volume after already being down 18% in 5 days suggests a real miss but possibly forced-selling tail. CNBC even ran a 'pullbacks as opportunity' piece naming Fluor — typically a contrarian fade-the-fade signal worth examining.
discoveries.what_confirms: If 8-K reveals project charge or guide cut on segment margins (Energy Solutions / Mission Solutions), magnitude is justified
discoveries.what_kills: If miss is purely NuScale mark-to-market noise with core E&C backlog and margins intact, the move is overdone and bounce-prone
discoveries.what_to_learn: When CNBC publishes a 'buy the dip' piece on the same day as the dip, it's a tell that retail attention is high — adds reflexivity risk in either direction; needs catalyst-by-catalyst dissection rather than headline reading.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Earnings 8-K with no impairment/restructuring code (no 2.05/2.06) suggests miss is operational not write-down driven; 5.07 is shareholder vote (annual meeting) — benign
research_pointers: Read FLR press release for segment margin detail and NuScale equity-method gain/loss
Compare new backlog and book-to-bill vs prior quarter — degradation here is the key bear signal
Check whether FY guidance was cut, reaffirmed, or maintained
Compare reaction to peer KBR if it reports — is this idiosyncratic or sector

discoveries.name: Globus Medical
discoveries.setup: Earnings reaction with 5-day decline of -16% — multi-day distribution
discoveries.thesis: Already down 16% over 5 days into print, then -9.6% on 4x volume = compounded disappointment. NuVasive integration concerns or competitive pressure from spine peers likely culprits. The cumulative magnitude (~25% off recent highs) suggests a guide cut or structural concern, not just a one-quarter miss.
discoveries.what_confirms: FY2026 revenue guide cut, deceleration in Enabling Tech (robotics) revenue, or commentary on competitive pressure
discoveries.what_kills: If miss is supply/seasonality with reiterated guide, severity is overdone and biased toward mean reversion
discoveries.what_to_learn: Stocks already down meaningfully into a print and then gapping down further on the print is a 'distribution before disaster' pattern — institutions often have information advantage; fade-the-drop is lower-probability than waiting.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Routine earnings 8-K but cumulative 5-day -16% prior to print suggests the market was already discounting bad news
research_pointers: Spine market share data vs Stryker, Medtronic spine division
Excelsius GPS robotic placement trends
Read prepared remarks for any mention of pricing, hospital capex, or 510k delays

discoveries.name: BILL Holdings
discoveries.setup: Earnings reaction with simultaneous restructuring announcement (2.05) — cost-cut narrative
discoveries.thesis: +8.6% on 3x volume despite an item 2.05 (exit/disposal costs) is the interesting part. Market is reading the restructuring as a positive — likely a cost reset that supports margin expansion. BILL has been a battered SaaS name; cost discipline + decent print is the typical 'bounce off floor' setup.
discoveries.what_confirms: Restructuring sized to drive >100bps operating margin lift; FY guide raised on EPS even if revenue muted
discoveries.what_kills: If restructuring relates to deteriorating segment (e.g. Divvy/spend-and-expense), it signals demand weakness not efficiency — bounce fades
discoveries.what_to_learn: Item 2.05 in the same 8-K as a positive earnings reaction usually means 'painful but pre-announced cost reset'; market often rewards this in beaten-down SaaS names. Distinguish 'good 2.05' (cost reset) from 'bad 2.05' (segment exit due to failure).
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 2.05 alongside positive market reaction implies the restructuring is being interpreted as efficiency-driven, not distress
research_pointers: Read 8-K exhibit for headcount reduction size and cost-savings target
Check if cuts are concentrated in any single segment
Take rate trend on core BILL platform vs Divvy

discoveries.name: Oshkosh Corporation
discoveries.setup: Earnings miss in industrials with 5-day -10.8% — defense/access/vocational mix concern
discoveries.thesis: Likely a guide-down driven by USPS NGDV delivery delays or access equipment (JLG) softness as nonresidential construction wobbles. Industrials sector is already weak today (XLI -0.39%). Magnitude in line with typical earnings disappointment; probably not overdone but no rush to catch.
discoveries.what_confirms: Access segment guide cut, NGDV margin commentary negative, or defense backlog flat
discoveries.what_kills: If miss is purely tariff timing/working capital with backlog growing, sets up a Q3 recovery
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Two 8-Ks same day (earnings + 5.07 annual meeting) — clean signal it's an earnings move, not governance
research_pointers: Access segment book-to-bill — leading indicator for JLG cycle
NGDV production ramp commentary
Compare to peer TEX or MTW reactions

discoveries.name: ESCO Technologies
discoveries.setup: Earnings reaction in defense/industrial-tech name on light volume
discoveries.thesis: Volume multiple only 1.45x is unusually low for a -9% drop, suggesting a high-quality holder base just trimming on a guide that was reset rather than panic. ESE typically trades at premium multiple; any miss compresses materially. Not a clear opportunity but watch for stabilization.
discoveries.what_confirms: Test & Measurement segment weakness or specific defense program slip
discoveries.what_kills: If the print is actually fine and selling is just multiple compression on an expensive name, it could continue lower without news
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Routine earnings 8-K; low volume on -9% drop signals concentrated ownership distribution rather than broad capitulation
research_pointers: Aerospace/Defense segment organic growth
Backlog trend
Whether multiple compression alone explains the move (compare prior PE to peers)

discoveries.name: Assured Guaranty
discoveries.setup: Earnings + day-after Reg FD disclosure — sequential 8-Ks suggest something developing
discoveries.thesis: Two filings in two days (earnings 5/7, then 7.01 Reg FD on 5/8) plus a -9.75% drop is unusual for a financial guarantee insurer that typically grinds. The follow-up 7.01 the day after earnings often signals clarification or correction of guidance — worth digging into the FD disclosure.
discoveries.what_confirms: If the 7.01 disclosure relates to a specific exposure (PR debt, healthcare bonds, or new loss reserve), magnitude is justified
discoveries.what_kills: If 7.01 is benign (investor day slides, dividend reaffirmation), -9.75% is overdone for a defensive insurer
discoveries.what_to_learn: Sequential 8-Ks within 24 hours (especially earnings + 7.01 next-day) often signal management clarifying a market misread or providing additional disclosure under counsel — always read the second filing carefully.
discoveries.catalyst: Q1 2026 earnings + next-day Reg FD disclosure
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Two 8-Ks 24 hours apart, second being Reg FD, suggests material clarification or additional info post-print
research_pointers: Read both 8-K exhibits — particularly the 5/8 Reg FD content
Check for any new loss reserve or downgrade exposure
Adjusted book value per share trajectory — primary AGO valuation metric

discoveries.name: Texas Roadhouse
discoveries.setup: Quality compounder earnings beat with item 8.01 (likely capital return announcement)
discoveries.thesis: 13% on a quality compounder like TXRH is large but not unprecedented for a clean beat with positive comps acceleration. The 8.01 alongside 2.02 often means dividend hike or buyback authorization. In a tape worried about consumer health, a Texas Roadhouse beat carries strong positive read-through.
discoveries.what_confirms: Same-store sales >5%, traffic positive, beef cost commentary stable
discoveries.what_kills: If beat is purely pricing-driven with traffic decelerating, multiple expansion may not stick
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Earnings + 8.01 suggests beat plus capital return / corporate development announcement
research_pointers: Comp traffic vs pricing breakdown
Beef commodity cost hedge position
Read 8.01 exhibit for capital allocation news

discoveries.name: MaxLinear
discoveries.setup: 20% rip on no 8-K and only 1.05x volume — sympathy with semi rotation
discoveries.thesis: No earnings filing, no 8-K, and volume barely above average despite a 20% move = either delayed filing of overnight news or pure sympathy with the AVGO/AMD/Micron memory-CPU rotation theme. Five-day +28% suggests this has been bid for days. The low volume multiple is the suspicious tell — moves this large should have institutional flow.
discoveries.what_confirms: Late-filed 8-K with new design win, M&A speculation, or guide raise; or sustained inclusion in the 'next leg' AI semi names
discoveries.what_kills: If rally fully reverses Monday on no follow-through news, this was retail-momentum/squeeze without fundamental backing
discoveries.what_to_learn: When a stock moves 20%+ on volume below 1.5x its average, the move is often technical/squeeze/retail rather than institutional repricing — these tend to fade or chop unless news hits.
discoveries.catalyst: Likely sympathy with semi rotation (Intel/AMD/Micron) — no company-specific 8-K
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals + 1.05x volume on +20% suggests squeeze/sympathy, not institutional repricing
research_pointers: Check for short interest level (squeeze candidate?)
Recent analyst notes — has there been an upgrade?
Watch for next earnings date — pre-positioning?
Connectivity/broadband design-win news flow

discoveries.name: Penguin Solutions
discoveries.setup: AI/memory infrastructure sympathy mover — 5-day +39% with no 8-K
discoveries.thesis: PENG (former SMART Global) provides memory and AI infrastructure solutions; a +39% 5-day move into a Micron/memory parabolic week makes thematic sense. But 1.48x volume on +11.75% is moderate and the cumulative move now looks parabolic. Late entrants are buying the theme, not the company.
discoveries.what_confirms: Memory chip pricing data continues higher; PENG announces design win or large customer ramp
discoveries.what_kills: Memory sentiment cools or PENG-specific execution stumble — late thematic buyers exit fast
discoveries.what_to_learn: Stocks that piggy-back a thematic narrative (here: memory/AI infra) without their own catalyst tend to be the LAST to rally and FIRST to crack — useful as a sentiment thermometer for the broader theme.
discoveries.catalyst: Sympathy with memory/AI infrastructure theme (Micron parabolic rally)
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: No 8-K; Micron/memory theme is up huge per news section
research_pointers: PENG revenue exposure to memory pricing
Penguin Computing AI cluster business momentum
Use as proxy/leading indicator for memory theme exhaustion

discoveries.name: LifeStance Health
discoveries.setup: Earnings disappointment in mental health services on heavy volume
discoveries.thesis: Mental health services have been under pressure on payor mix, telehealth normalization, and clinician productivity. -12.7% on 3.7x volume after only a +2.5% 5-day suggests genuine surprise — likely guide cut or margin miss. Sector context (XLV -0.89%) reinforces.
discoveries.what_confirms: EBITDA guide cut, clinician churn higher, payor rate pressure called out
discoveries.what_kills: If miss is investment-driven (clinician hiring) with revenue strong, longer-term thesis intact
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Earnings + Reg FD disclosure (often guidance update); heavy volume confirms broad institutional re-rating
research_pointers: Visits per clinician trend
Payor rate negotiation commentary
Center-level EBITDA margin

discoveries.name: Dropbox
discoveries.setup: Mature SaaS earnings beat — typical 'cheap stock that didn't break' reaction
discoveries.thesis: DBX has been treated as a value/buyback story; a clean print with stable paying users + strong FCF + buyback authorization easily produces 15% in this name. The Reg FD (7.01) likely accompanies guide reaffirmation or buyback news.
discoveries.what_confirms: FCF guide raised, paying user growth >0%, AI product (Dash) traction commentary
discoveries.what_kills: If beat is FX/one-time and paying users still declining, rally fades within days
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Standard earnings beat 8-K with Reg FD addition (typical for guide/buyback announcement)
research_pointers: Paying user net adds
FCF guide path
Buyback pace and remaining authorization

discoveries.name: Kennametal
discoveries.setup: Short-cycle industrial earnings miss reflecting weak end markets
discoveries.thesis: KMT is a tooling/cutting bellwether tied to manufacturing PMI and energy capex. -12.6% likely reflects guide cut on weak GE Aerospace tooling demand or oil & gas slowdown despite XLE relative stability. Read-through to broader short-cycle industrials.
discoveries.what_confirms: Energy/Aerospace segment guide cut, FX headwind sized
discoveries.what_kills: If miss is pure FX or one-time charge with end-markets stable, sets up bounce
research_pointers: Industrial / Energy / Aerospace segment organic growth
Read-through to peer SWK, MSM, FAST
Manufacturing PMI correlation

catalyst_chains.primary_event: Memory chip parabolic rally (Micron +30% week, AMD/Intel double-digit) creating thematic spillover
FLR Q1 miss in E&C sector

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump threatens EU with 'much higher' tariffs if no deal by July 4
trump_signals.note: EU-exposed autos and consumer goods names should be watched — but mid-cap impact most likely via auto parts (BWA, AXL) and EU-revenue industrials. VIX +1.7% despite SPY up suggests some hedging is occurring around this deadline.

ai_announcements.headline: Cloudflare cuts 1,100 jobs citing AI efficiency gains while revenue hits record high
ai_announcements.summary: Cloudflare's CEO attributes layoffs (primarily support roles) to AI productivity gains. Notable as a high-profile labor-displacement narrative from a profitable company.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: LifeStance is a clinician-driven mental health provider; AI labor displacement narrative doesn't directly hit therapy services. The drop is almost certainly driven by today's 8-K earnings (Item 2.02) plus 7.01 disclosure, not the Cloudflare news. AI could even reduce LFST's admin overhead.
research_pointers: Read LFST Q1 2026 earnings release and guidance
Check whether 7.01 FD disclosure relates to provider productivity / payer mix
Move is earnings-driven, not AI-news-driven; not a Michael setup unless guidance cites AI

ai_announcements.headline: OpenAI launches new voice intelligence features in API
ai_announcements.summary: OpenAI rolling out voice features positioned at customer service, education, creator platforms.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: BILL move is clearly earnings-driven (8-K with 2.02 and 2.05 restructuring). Not connected to OpenAI voice news.
research_pointers: BILL move is earnings + restructuring, not AI announcement reaction

ai_announcements.headline: Anthropic and OpenAI announce new joint ventures targeting enterprise AI deployment
ai_announcements.summary: Mentioned in podcast summary alongside SAP's $1B Prior Labs acquisition. Enterprise AI consolidation theme. NOTE: This is an Anthropic-related call, flagged for separate bias grading.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: DBX rip is earnings-driven (8-K 2.02). Enterprise AI narrative could be tailwind via Dropbox Dash, but today's move is fundamentals, not the Anthropic/OpenAI JV news.
research_pointers: DBX Q1 2026 print details — likely the real catalyst
Dash adoption metrics if disclosed

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: Most large moves in the market_data are clearly earnings-driven (8-K Item 2.02 filings on 5/7-5/8: FLR, LFST, GMED, KMT, OSK, ESE, CARG down; SYNA, DBX, NSIT, TXRH, BILL, NHC, ASTH, WMG up). MXL, CALY, PENG moves also lack any clear AI-news linkage. None of today's AI announcements (Cloudflare layoffs, OpenAI voice API, Perplexity Mac app, Anthropic/OpenAI enterprise JVs) appear to be the proximate cause of any listed move. Quiet night for the AI-news-vs-stock-move thesis; no high-conviction Michael setups identified.
